body.body:
From Akien 2026-04-14: 'we run tests. a run all with local models and another with all cloud models. and we sample different LLMs for quality. that's a ticket all by itself. but we are aiming for less and less cloud.'

## Shape

Experimentation framework for comparing voice actor configurations on the same decision blob. Run the SAME decision through:
- All-local configuration (qwen2.5:7b, llama3.2, etc.)
- All-cloud configuration (current LLM)
- Mixed configurations (e.g. local for routine, cloud for complex)
- Different cloud LLMs at different price points (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus, gpt-4o-mini, DeepSeek, etc.)

Score quality, capture outputs, build a body of evidence on which configuration delivers acceptable Igor voice at what cost.

## Why standalone

Akien explicitly named this as 'a ticket all by itself.' It's the measurement infrastructure that informs the migration. Without it the parallel-voice-actors comparison from T-reasoning-voice-split has no way to score quality.

## Existing work to check

- wild_igor/igor/cognition/inference_gateway.py — already supports model_override (added in D359 reading-extract-via-gateway)
- D360 reading-model-benchmark — same shape, different domain (reading extraction). Could share infrastructure. Existing 7-pass benchmark covers gpt-4o-mini, llama3.2:1b, qwen2.5:7b, DeepSeek R1 distill, DeepSeek R1 cloud, Haiku, Sonnet. THIS TICKET IS THE VOICE EQUIVALENT.
- benchmarks/ directory in lab/
- tools/budget.py — cost tracking, latency tracking
- inference_ollama.py for local model calls
- inference_openrouter.py for cloud routing

## Open design questions

- Quality scoring: human (Akien) vibe-check, automated similarity to past Igor responses, third-LLM judge, or a mix?
- Sample size per configuration before drawing conclusions?
- Production gating: when does a configuration get promoted from 'experiment' to 'default for tier X turns'?
- Cost tracking format: per-call, per-configuration, rolled up?
- Stable test set vs sampling live traffic?

## North star

'We are aiming for less and less cloud.' Each successful local-config experiment shrinks cloud burn. Eventually most voice production runs locally with cloud only as a fallback for hard cases.

## Dependencies

- Parent: T-reasoning-voice-split
- Reuses infrastructure from D360 reading-model-benchmark (same shape)
- Co-evolves with T-decision-blob-schema (the input format both lanes consume)
body.title: A/B test framework for voice actors — local vs cloud, sample LLMs at different price points
body.result: VoiceABFramework: GraphVoiceActor (word graph G37) + LLMVoiceActor (gateway) compete per blob. Winner selected, loser trains graph via reinforce_text. Graduation tracked via stats(). JSONL logging. 22 tests.
